sanskrit: यत्साङ्ख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते। एकं साङ्ख्यं च योगं च यः पश्यति स पश्यति॥
transliteration: yat sāṅkhyaiḥ prāpyate sthānaṃ tad yogair api gamyate | ekaṃ sāṅkhyaṃ ca yogaṃ ca yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati ||
literal_meaning: What is attained by the followers of Sāṅkhya is also reached by the yogins; he who sees that Sāṅkhya and Yoga are one, he sees (truly).
contextual_meaning: The mind that insists on choosing between 'letting go' and 'engaging' is trapped in a false dichotomy; both paths, when authentically lived, converge on the same inner posture of non-clinging presence.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance resolved; the rigid either/or ego-structure relaxes into a both/and perspective, reducing decision-paralysis.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out professional believes she must either quit her high-pressure job (renunciation) or keep grinding (action); the verse reframes the dilemma into finding mindful engagement within the same role.
anchor_text: Letting go and engaging meet in the same quiet, open heart.
